phase_statuses.title: Add release ledger schema + supersede fields
phase_statuses.objective: Persist explicit release/distribution records and model supersede metadata on replaced revisions.
phase_statuses.evidence_expectation: schema + model changes exist and are referenced by tests/service load paths.

acceptance_criteria.text: `ensure_document_control_tables()` creates the release ledger table and indexes, and adds revision supersede columns additively.
`ControlledRevision.as_dict()` includes supersede metadata fields populated from DB rows.

phase_statuses.title: Implement explicit publish action + supersede semantics
phase_statuses.objective: Implement a publish action that makes an approved revision effective, writes ledger rows, and supersedes the previous effective revision without using lifecycle `obsolete`.
phase_statuses.evidence_expectation: service unit test asserts gating + ledger rows + supersede semantics.

acceptance_criteria.text: Publish rejects when the revision is not `approved_pending_effective` (approval completion is not treated as release).
evidence: Service: publish_revision() gates on approved_pending_effective in backend/services/document_control/service.py

acceptance_criteria.text: Publish enforces the training gate: missing `training_compliance_service` or missing requirements/records/certification blocks publish (no bypass).
evidence: Service: publish_revision() enforces training gate via training_compliance_service in backend/services/document_control/service.py

acceptance_criteria.text: Publish writes release ledger rows including mode/actor/time/target + replaced revision id, and makes the revision `effective` with `effective_at_ms`.
When a new revision is published over an existing effective one, the previous effective becomes `superseded` (not `obsolete`) with `superseded_at_ms` and `superseded_by_revision_id`.
evidence: Service: previous effective revision updated to superseded with superseded_* fields in backend/services/document_control/service.py

phase_statuses.title: Expose publish API + required approval endpoints
phase_statuses.objective: Provide explicit API endpoints to drive the flow without legacy `/transitions`.
phase_statuses.evidence_expectation: API unit test uses explicit endpoints end-to-end.

acceptance_criteria.text: Router exposes explicit publish endpoint and required approval endpoints; legacy `/transitions` is not available as a working path.

phase_statuses.title: Update unit + API tests
phase_statuses.objective: Prove the controlled release contract and ledger behavior via the required pytest targets.
phase_statuses.evidence_expectation: `test-report.md` contains a pass verdict and command output summary.
phase_statuses.notes: - Updated service/API tests for explicit publish, release ledger, supersede semantics, and existing publish-time distribution coupling.
